answer:
If `CHKIN` changes, any of the routines that call it might break: `ADDCOM`, `APPNDC`, `APPNDD`, `APPNDI`, `ATTCMP`, `AZLCPO`, `BADKPV`, `BBOARD`, `BGROUP_1`, `BLTFRM`, `BODC2N`, `BODC2S`, `BODDEF`, `BODEUL`, `BODFND`, `BODMAT`, `BODN2C`, `BODS2C`, `BODVAR`, `BODVCD` [file:trcpkg.f:499-499].

`CHKIN` informs the SPICELIB error handling mechanism that a routine has been entered [file:trcpkg.f:502-504]. It pushes the module name onto a stack for traceback purposes [file:trcpkg.f:590-593].
